mapping.a_element: ordered stages indexed by successive secondary statistics
mapping.b_element: successive parameter values along the continuation path

mapping.a_element: conditioning each stage's calibration on acceptance at all preceding stages (carrying history forward)
mapping.b_element: updating the prior with newly sampled data at each nearby parameter value (carrying the posterior forward)

mapping.a_element: calibrated acceptance-region level controlling stagewise Type I error
mapping.b_element: log Bayes factor threshold triggering dense resampling

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic 'sequential statistical decision that carries prior stages forward'; the actual machinery differs. Paper A is frequentist sequential goodness-of-fit testing whose transferable engine is conditional calibration of acceptance regions giving a multiplicative Type I error decomposition over a fixed ordered set of statistics on one sample, whereas Paper B is Bayesian conjugate (Dirichlet-multinomial) posterior updating along a parameter continuation with a log-Bayes-factor change detector acting on attractor basins in phase space. Different estimation principles (Type I error control vs. Bayesian belief updating), different objects, and a 'sequence' running over different axes mean no role-for-role governing-process mapping holds.
